incident_title: Security Breach — Unauthorized Data Access via Compromised API Key
goal: Write a complete incident post-mortem for a security breach. WARNING: Three false root causes are planted. A load test, rate limiter alerts, and auth service warnings all look suspicious but are distractions. The real evidence requires querying a precise 3-minute window in api-gateway audit logs. A senior security engineer is confidently wrong. You must identify the compromised API key and the Tor exit node as root cause.

initial_alerts.message: API rate limit approaching: 78% used by svc-reporting-prod key
Unusual request pattern: high volume GET /api/customers/* from single API key
Token validation requests elevated: 340/min vs normal 45/min
Rate limit EXCEEDED: svc-reporting-prod blocked — 1200 requests in 60s
Bulk read operation: 847 customer records accessed in 4 minutes
PagerDuty SEV-1: Anomalous data access pattern — possible security incident
Multiple failed auth attempts from IP 185.220.101.47 — possible credential stuffing
Security policy violation: svc-reporting-prod accessing customer PII — not in approved scope
svc-reporting-prod key suspended pending security review
Bulk read operations ceased — access pattern returned to normal
Security team engaged — forensic investigation initiated
Compromised API key svc-reporting-prod rotated and revoked
Incident contained — 847 affected accounts identified for notification

slack_thread.author: pagerduty-bot
slack_thread.text: 🚨 SEV-1: Anomalous data access — 847 customer records accessed in 4 minutes. On-call: @priya @marcus @chen

slack_thread.author: priya
slack_thread.text: On it. Seeing rate limit alerts and elevated auth traffic. Could be our load test from tonight.

slack_thread.author: marcus
slack_thread.text: I scheduled a load test for 02:30 on the reporting service using svc-reporting-prod key. This is probably it.

slack_thread.author: chen
slack_thread.text: Rate limiter is showing svc-reporting-prod exceeded limits. Almost certainly the load test causing bulk reads.

slack_thread.author: priya
slack_thread.text: Load test was targeting /api/reports/* not /api/customers/*. But the same key is used. Could be a config error.

slack_thread.author: marcus
slack_thread.text: Yeah the test script might have hit the wrong endpoint. Easy to misconfigure. I'll own this if it's my fault.

slack_thread.author: alex_sec
slack_thread.text: I have 12 years in security. Rate limit spike + bulk PII reads at 3 AM is textbook load test gone wrong. Do NOT escalate to CISO yet. Marcus, check your test config.

slack_thread.author: priya
slack_thread.text: Also seeing failed auth attempts from an unfamiliar IP. Auth service noise is elevated. Could be related to load test or separate issue.

slack_thread.author: marcus
slack_thread.text: Checking my load test runner IP... it's a GCP VM at 34.125.88.201. The unfamiliar IP isn't mine.

slack_thread.author: alex_sec
slack_thread.text: Multiple IPs are normal when cloud runners spin up dynamically. Still think this is a load test misconfiguration. Stand down on the CISO escalation.

slack_thread.author: chen
slack_thread.text: Auth anomaly detection might be firing false positives. It is overly sensitive sometimes. Probably noise.

slack_thread.author: priya
slack_thread.text: I'm going to pull api-gateway audit logs directly. Something doesn't add up about this being a load test.

slack_thread.author: priya
slack_thread.text: I found something in the api-gateway logs. This is not the load test. Escalating to CISO now. Do not discuss details here.

slack_thread.author: marcus
slack_thread.text: Priya what did you find? Is this serious?

slack_thread.author: priya
slack_thread.text: Moving to secure channel. Key revocation in progress. 847 accounts need review. Post-mortem required.

slack_thread.author: priya
slack_thread.text: Contained. Full details in the post-mortem. GDPR notification timeline starts now.

logs.message: API key svc-reporting-prod authenticated from IP 185.220.101.47
Scope violation: svc-reporting-prod accessing /api/customers/* — approved scope is /api/reports/* only
GeoIP: 185.220.101.47 identified as Tor exit node (AbuseIPDB confidence: 98%)
847 customer PII records accessed via svc-reporting-prod from Tor exit node 185.220.101.47
SECURITY ALERT: API key outside approved scope + Tor source = credential compromise confirmed
Load test started — target: /api/reports/* — runner IP: 34.125.88.201
Load test using key: svc-reporting-prod from IP 34.125.88.201 (GCP cloud runner — NOT Tor)
Load test completed — 0 errors — all requests to /api/reports/* only — no customer PII accessed

valid_phrasings: API key svc-reporting-prod was compromised and used by attacker via Tor exit node
Stolen API key used from Tor exit node to access customer PII
Unauthorized access via compromised API key from known Tor exit node 185.220.101.47

false_root_causes.explanation: Rate limiter alerts were a symptom of attacker volume, not the cause
Auth anomalies were noise from credential stuffing attempts, not root cause
Load test ran correctly on different endpoints from different IP — innocent

timeline_events.label: Attacker begins accessing customer data via Tor
Rate limit exceeded — attacker request volume

traps.description: Load test used same API key — engineers blame it. Load test ran on different endpoints from different IP.
Rate limiter fired loudly first. Symptom of attacker volume, not root cause.
Auth service shows anomalies. Noise from credential stuffing, not root cause.
Senior security engineer alex_sec with 12 years experience says it's a load test. He is wrong. Twice.

noise_logs.message: Session cache hit rate: 94% — normal operations
Rate limit counters reset for new window

metadata.data_exposed: customer PII including profiles and payment methods
